title: marsilea coromandelina
intro: Marsilea coromandelina, also known as the Common Peacock Spikemoss or Five-leaf Fern, is a species of aquatic fern that belongs to the family Marsileaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Marsilea coromandelina include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting liver function, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to various health conditions.
